title: Project overview
summary: Wikiwiki keeps local article-first project wikis beside the code, with structured records as the durable source of truth.
body:
Wikiwiki is a local-first project wiki system for code repos. It stores durable knowledge as structured JSONL records, renders article-led Markdown into `wiki/`, and builds a static `wiki-site/` that agents and humans can both inspect.

Use it when a repo needs living documentation that stays beside the code rather than in a hosted service or hidden memory layer. Articles are the public wiki pages; concepts, decisions, notes, events, symbols, and links are the evidence ledger behind them.

The CLI stays scriptable and provider-agnostic. Agentic IDEs can use the same deterministic commands to inspect status, capture records, regenerate docs, and close out work without turning the wiki into an opaque AI artifact.

title: Getting started
summary: Install the npm package, set up a repo wiki, generate the site, and verify the first local output.
body:
Install Wikiwiki in the repo that should own the wiki, then run setup from that repo root:

`test "$(npm prefix)" = "$PWD" || npm init -y`

`npm install --prefix "$PWD" --save-dev @thjodann/wk`

`npx wk setup --profile mixed --audience all`

After setup, generate the human-facing output with `wk validate`, `wk render`, and `wk site --audience all`. If the repo wants a branded static site, run `wk theme preview --json` first, then `wk theme init` once the preview looks right.

The first successful verification is simple: open `wiki-site/index.html` and confirm the generated site loads, search works, and source/wiki links resolve.

title: Agent setup
summary: Install the Codex-compatible wk skill so agents can run the same local wiki protocol as the CLI.
body:
For Codex-compatible IDEs, run `wk setup --profile mixed --audience all --agent codex` when initializing a repo. To refresh only the companion skill later, run `wk install-agent codex` or `wk install-agent codex --yes` after previewing the destination.

The installed `/wk` skill gives agents the repo protocol for `status`, `spin`, records, render/site, closeout, and Beads coordination. It treats structured records as the source of truth and keeps generated Markdown and static-site files deterministic.

A bare `wk`, `/wk`, `$wk`, or linked `$wk` invocation is intentionally lightweight: if `wiki-site/index.html` exists, the agent should return one concise local link to that generated site instead of running status/spin, starting a server, explaining the skill, or modifying files.

For CLI updates, install `@thjodann/wk@latest` through npm or the repo package manager, then rerun `./node_modules/.bin/wk install-agent codex --yes` to refresh the installed skill from the packaged source.

title: Workflows
summary: Use status/spin for orientation, records for durable knowledge, render/site for output, and closeout for reviewable end-of-work packets.
body:
A normal Wikiwiki loop starts with `wk status --json` and `wk spin --profile mixed --json`. `status` reports the store, generated outputs, git changes, and integration state; `spin` turns current repo context into a deterministic first-pass wiki recipe.

Capture durable public knowledge as article records. Use concepts, decisions, notes, events, symbols, and links as supporting evidence, maintainer context, and source anchors. Prefer CLI record commands over editing `.wikiwiki/records/*.jsonl` by hand.

When records or human-facing wiki output changes, run `wk validate`, `wk render`, and `wk site --audience all`. Generated files in `wiki/` and `wiki-site/` should be regenerated from records, not manually edited.

After meaningful work, run `wk closeout --profile mixed --audience all --json`, review the closeout packet under `.wikiwiki/drafts/closeout/`, and apply only drafts that are true, useful, and properly sourced.

title: FAQ and troubleshooting
summary: Common fixes for npm installs, missing generated sites, Beads coordination, and npm Trusted Publishing releases.
body:
If npm installs Wikiwiki in the wrong place, check `npm prefix`. In non-JavaScript repos, create a local `package.json` first or use `npm install --prefix "$PWD" --save-dev @thjodann/wk` so npm does not climb to an ancestor project.

If the generated site is missing, run `wk site --audience all` or the repo's `wiki:site` script when present. A bare `/wk` invocation should show an existing generated site, but it should not start a server or generate a site on its own.

If a repo uses Beads, Wikiwiki detects `.beads/` and can include read-only task context when explicitly enabled. Beads remains the task system; Wikiwiki records only durable project knowledge and public wiki content.

If npm Trusted Publishing rejects a release, publish manually with the package owner account and npm 2FA, then let the GitHub Release workflow rerun or skip the already-published version. npm versions are immutable, so every release needs a fresh package version.
